name: Source Fidelity review for Chromatin And Histone Modulation bundle
summary: The staged source, study, finding, and outlook records match the cited PubMed and PMC records closely enough for publication review. The KAT7, SIRT6, SRT1720, spermidine, beta-hydroxybutyrate, H3K4me3, UTX-1/H3K27, and butyrate/tributyrin claims preserve source boundaries, model organism or disease-model context, endpoint type, and material quantitative caveats.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged quantitative lifespan statements for SIRT6 overexpression, SRT1720, and beta-hydroxybutyrate were checked against the cited abstracts or full-text records and stay within the reported effect sizes and significance caveats.
findings.why_it_matters: These values anchor the animal_signal stage; preserving the source-level caveats prevents the outlook from implying stronger or broader support than the studies provide.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required; keep the current caveats on sex specificity, mean-versus-median lifespan, and model-organism scope through editorial approval.

findings.claim_or_issue: The butyrate/tributyrin source is a mitochondrial-deficiency mouse study rather than a normal-aging intervention study.
findings.why_it_matters: The staged finding already frames the result as disease-model evidence with epigenetic rescue and does not use it as direct proof of broad geroscience efficacy.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required; retain the disease-model and mechanism-overlap caveats during editorial approval.
